claim_boundary: Sigma one-slope SD-target coverage measured at SR475 (Fisher+Curie verified, DGP<->model aligned); Wald is the trustworthy channel (near-nominal 0.94-0.99); profile coverage is censoring-suspect (non-finite profiles are the high-shrinkage reps Wald misses) and must not be read as a reliability channel; bias is g=8 shrinkage; this is a diagnostic coverage measurement that does NOT promote the linked cell coverage_status off planned, does not advance interval_status, and does not promote supported, REML, AI-REML, bridge, or public support.

Bivariate q2 slope-only SD and correlation coverage measured at SR475 (Fisher+Curie verified, DGP<->model aligned); a NEGATIVE result: Wald 0.87-0.91 and profile 0.90-0.92 both under-cover the 0.95 nominal (~4-5 MCSE below) under g=8 SD shrinkage (narrow/off-centre intervals; the unbiased correlation target still under-covers from a ~27% Wald SE deficit on 8 levels); intervals are NOT reliable for these targets; this measurement does NOT promote the linked cell coverage_status off planned, does not advance interval_status, and does not promote supported, REML, AI-REML, bridge, or public support; the excluded animal/cor and relmat/cor holdouts stay unmeasured.
